corpus_description: Fifty recent arXiv papers (cs.LG / cs.CL / cs.AI) with PDF + markdown sidecars.

requirements.description: Each query must answer correctly with the index and fail without it. At least two require semantic recall where query wording does not appear verbatim in answering chunks.

queries.title: SkillOpt external skill memory
queries.query: In my indexed research library, which work treats an agent skill as trainable external memory updated through scored rollouts rather than weight fine-tuning?

queries.title: AutoResearchClaw failure-as-signal
queries.query: Which indexed paper treats failed experiment executions as informative feedback rather than terminal errors in a multi-agent autonomous research workflow?
queries.semantic_note: Semantic paraphrase of the Pivot/Refine loop; avoids exact 'pivot-or-refine' phrasing from the abstract.

queries.title: Sleep-like LM consolidation
queries.query: What do my indexed papers say about sleep-like consolidation that writes recent context into fast weights before clearing the KV cache?
queries.semantic_note: Keyword anchor on Language Models Need Sleep (2605.26099).

queries.title: Retrieval + generation synthesis
queries.query: Across my indexed arXiv library, how do recent works combine retrieval with generation for grounded question answering or detection?
queries.semantic_note: Cross-document synthesis; terms are corpus-specific (2605.26019 Chilean ToS RAG + 2605.25988 medical checker RAG).

queries.title: Info-Synth preference queries
queries.query: Which indexed study generates comparison questions using an information-theoretic selection criterion when obtaining trustworthy pairwise user labels is costly?
queries.semantic_note: Paraphrases Info-Synth; avoids verbatim 'mutual-information objective' from the abstract.
